description: Show current commit streak as ambient data in today output — positive framing only, never nag

userStories.title: streak_query: compute current commit streak from DB
userStories.description: Add `query_streak(conn, exclude_weekends: bool) -> anyhow::Result<u32>` in `src/query.rs`. A streak is the count of consecutive calendar days (local time) ending today on which at least one commit was recorded in `git_activity`. If today has no commits yet, the streak is the count of consecutive days ending yesterday (streak is still alive until EOD). Day boundaries are local-time midnight. Returns 0 if no commits ever recorded.
acceptanceCriteria: Returns 0 when git_activity is empty
Returns 1 when commits exist only on today
Returns 1 when commits exist only on yesterday (no commits today yet)
Returns N for N consecutive days including today
Returns N for N consecutive days ending yesterday (today not yet committed)
Gap of 1 day resets streak to days-after-gap count only
exclude_weekends=true: Saturday and Sunday are skipped — a commit on Friday + Monday = streak of 2
exclude_weekends=false (default): weekends counted normally
Uses local time for day boundaries (not UTC)
Only event_type='commit' rows count — branch_switch and merge do not extend a streak
Unit tests in tests/streak_test.rs cover all cases above

userStories.title: ActivitySummary carries streak field
userStories.description: Add `streak_days: u32` field to `ActivitySummary` in `src/query.rs`. In `run_query()` in `main.rs`, call `query_streak()` after `query_activity()` and populate the field. Pass `config.streak_exclude_weekends` (new config field, default false) to `query_streak`.
acceptanceCriteria: ActivitySummary has `streak_days: u32`
All existing ActivitySummary constructions in tests compile (compiler-guided)
run_query() populates streak_days from query_streak()
Standup command path also populates streak_days (or 0 if skipped — it is not shown in standup output)
No panic if DB query fails — log warn, default to streak_days=0

userStories.title: pretty output: streak shown in today header
userStories.description: In `render_summary_to_string()` in `src/output.rs`, when `summary.period_label == "Today"` and `summary.streak_days >= 1`, append the streak to the summary line. Format: `12-day streak` (plain, no emoji). Never show any text about broken streaks or missed days.
acceptanceCriteria: When period='Today' and streak_days=1: summary line ends with `  1-day streak`
When period='Today' and streak_days=7: summary line ends with `  7-day streak`
When period='Today' and streak_days=0: no streak text shown
When period='This Week' or 'This Month': no streak text shown
Streak text is dimmed (use `.dimmed()` from colored crate)
Text never contains words: broken, missed, lost, reset, end, over
Unit test in tests/output_test.rs: streak_days=5 → contains '5-day streak'
Unit test: streak_days=0 → does not contain 'streak'
Unit test: period='This Week' with streak_days=5 → does not contain 'streak'

userStories.title: JSON output: streak_days field in JsonSummary
userStories.description: Add `streak_days: u32` to `JsonSummary` struct in `src/output.rs`. Populate from `summary.streak_days` in `render_json()`. Field is always present (not skipped when zero).
acceptanceCriteria: JsonSummary has `streak_days: u32`
`blackbox today --format json` output contains `"streak_days": N`
streak_days=0 still serializes as `"streak_days": 0` (not omitted)
Unit test: render_json with streak_days=3 → JSON string contains '"streak_days": 3'
Existing render_json tests still pass

userStories.title: config: streak_exclude_weekends field
userStories.description: Add `streak_exclude_weekends: bool` to `Config` in `src/config.rs` with `#[serde(default)]` (default false). No interactive prompt — manual config edit only.
acceptanceCriteria: Config struct has `streak_exclude_weekends: bool` with `#[serde(default)]`
Config::default() sets streak_exclude_weekends = false
Existing configs without the field load without error (serde default)
Config round-trip test: serialize + deserialize preserves streak_exclude_weekends=true
All existing Config constructions in tests compile

userStories.title: integration test: today command includes streak in output
userStories.description: Add an integration test using `assert_cmd` that wires a real DB with known commit history, calls `blackbox today --format json`, and asserts the streak_days field is correct. Also assert pretty output contains the streak string when streak > 0.
acceptanceCriteria: Test sets up temp DB with commits on today + 2 prior consecutive days
JSON output: streak_days == 3
Pretty output contains '3-day streak'
Test with only today's commit: streak_days == 1, pretty output contains '1-day streak'
Test uses tempfile for DB, sets XDG env vars to temp dirs
Test does not depend on system git config or real repos

userStories.title: Update README.md and AGENTS.md
userStories.description: Update project documentation to reflect new feature. README.md: add new command to Commands table if applicable, update feature descriptions. AGENTS.md: add new source files to Architecture tree, update Key Patterns if new patterns introduced.
acceptanceCriteria: README.md Commands table includes new command (if a CLI command was added)
README.md describes the new feature/capability
AGENTS.md Architecture src/ tree lists any new .rs files
AGENTS.md Key Dependencies updated if new crates added
userStories.notes: This must be the LAST story executed. Reference README.md and AGENTS.md in project root.
